name: Àreu - Alins - Port de Cabús - Alins - Àreu — Ruta por el Parque Natural del Alto Pirineo
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Alins are situated in the heart of the Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for riders. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, dense forests, and high mountain passes like Port de Cabús. Mountain biking here involves navigating varied terrain, from winding forest paths to open mountain tracks, often within the Parc Natural de l'Alt Pirineu. Best mountain bike trails around Alins The most popular mountain bike trail is Àreu - Alins - Port de Cabús - Alins - Àreu — Ruta por el Parque Natural del Alto Pirineo, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the Alt Pirineu Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ribera de Cardós, Pla de Negua, and Tudela Pass Loop, a difficult 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents across varied mountain terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Tor – Port de Cabús loop from Bixessarri, a 51.4 miles (82.7 km) trail leading through high mountain landscapes and passes, often completed in about 9 hours 34 minutes. Mountain biking around Alins is defined by high mountain passes, dense forest sections, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on challenging routes. The routes in Alins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alins?
A: Alins offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available. These range from easier paths to very challenging high-mountain expeditions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alins?
A: Mountain biking in Alins is characterized by significant elevation changes, dense forests, and high mountain passes like Port de Cabús. You'll navigate varied terrain, from winding forest paths to open mountain tracks, often within the Parc Natural de l'Alt Pirineu. The routes generally focus on challenging climbs and descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Alins?
A: While many routes in Alins are challenging, there are approximately 10 easier mountain bike trails available. These provide a good starting point for those new to the area or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Alins?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31817643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Ribera de Cardós&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 43.6 km route features substantial elevation gain and varied mountain terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20526711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tor – Camino de Tor to Port de Cabús loop from Alt Pirineu Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.3 km difficult trail with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through the Parc Natural de l'Alt Pirineu, offering stunning natural landscapes. You might encounter high mountain passes like Port de Cabús, dense forest sections, and river valleys. For specific points of interest, you can find various huts and refuges in the area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinet Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3026639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugi Certascan&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as landmarks or rest stops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alins?
A: Given the high mountain passes and potential for snow, the best time for mountain biking in Alins is typically from late spring through early autumn. Always check local conditions and weather forecasts, especially for higher elevations, as seasonal closures or difficult conditions can occur outside these months.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Parc Natural de l'Alt Pirineu?
A: While general access to the Parc Natural de l'Alt Pirineu for mountain biking is usually permitted, it's always advisable to check with the park's official website or local information centers for any specific regulations, especially concerning protected areas or during certain seasons. Some areas might have restrictions to protect wildlife or maintain trail integrity.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Alins?
A: Yes, Alins offers some family-friendly mountain bike trails. Approximately 10 of the routes are rated as easy, making them more suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. These trails typically have less elevation gain and smoother surfaces.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trailheads in Alins?
A: Specific parking information for individual trailheads is often detailed on the respective route pages on komoot. Generally, villages like Alins and Àreu, or starting points for popular loops, will have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the tour details for the specific route you plan to ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alins?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alins are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging nature and the stunning high-mountain landscapes of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any bike parks or dedicated mountain biking facilities near Alins?
A: Yes, for those interested in dedicated facilities, there are bike parks in the wider region. Notably, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6372024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallnord Bike Park Pal Arinsal&lt;/a&gt; is a significant bike park nearby, offering a range of downhill and enduro trails. You can also find facilities like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7741987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CX / BMX Circuit Arinsal&lt;/a&gt; for specific training.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Alins?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Alins are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952570060/ribera-de-cardos-pla-de-negua-and-tudela-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera de Cardós, Pla de Negua, and Tudela Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tor – Port de Cabús loop from Bixessarri&lt;/a&gt;, which offer extensive circular rides through the mountain landscape.
tips: A beautiful, mixed surface climb from the village of Alins up to the border between Spain and Andorra. The first 10km up to the village of Tor are on surfaced and concrete roads. After Tor the road changes to a wide gravel path, rough in places, that climbs up through the forest to the border in a series of switchbacks. As you climb, remember to keep taking a look behind as the view back down the valley into Spain is just stunning. Once you're at the summit and you've crossed into Andorra, the track turns to smooth tarmac for a fast and equally stunning descent into the village of Pal.
One of the mountain passes with the most spectacular views of Andorra, since in its final part the route opens up (be careful with the wind!) allowing us to see these beautiful panoramic views.

Beautiful tracks that connect Andorra from Port de Cabús to Vall Ferrera, Catalonia, through the mysterious village of Tor until reaching the road to Alins. The state of the track is very irregular, especially in the first few kilometers, with a lot of stone and big potholes: made with gravel I suffered a lot and did some sections walking. Recommended to do it with BTT.
This village is crazy beautiful and seems to have been stuck in a forgotten time. It's also called the murder village. But don't fear the locals are very nice. ;)
